title: CI/CD GitHub Actions — make test à chaque push
description: Créer .github/workflows/ci.yml qui exécute `make test` (Node + pytest) sur chaque push et PR. Empêche toute régression silencieuse. Ajouter un badge status dans le README.

title: FAQ schema JSON-LD sur les pages outils
description: Générer automatiquement un bloc <script type='application/ld+json'> FAQPage à partir des questions/réponses déjà présentes dans le body markdown de chaque outil. Rich results Google = CTR +15–30% estimé.

title: Pages pays Stripe programmatiques (UK, CA, AU, EU, IN)
description: Créer content/countries.yaml et un template intent_country.html pour générer 5–10 pages '/tools/stripe-fee-calculator/stripe-fees-[country]/' pré-remplies avec les taux locaux. Faible concurrence, fort volume longue traîne.

title: Remplacer og:image SVG par PNG statique
description: Créer /static/img/og.png (1200×630) pour le partage social. Facebook, LinkedIn et Twitter/X ne prévisualisent pas les SVG. Ajouter og:image:width et og:image:height dans base.html.

title: Header Content-Security-Policy dans nginx.conf
description: Ajouter un CSP restrictif dans nginx.conf : self pour scripts/styles, pagead2.googlesyndication.com pour AdSense, bloquer inline scripts non-nonce. Améliore sécurité et confiance AdSense.

title: Tests dynamiques — lire tools.yaml au lieu de listes hard-codées
description: Remplacer TOOL_SLUGS et INTENT_PAGES dans test_build.py par un chargement dynamique depuis content/tools.yaml et content/intent_pages.yaml. Élimine la désynchronisation garantie à chaque ajout d'outil.

title: Étendre la couverture tests JS à tous les outils (22)
description: Ajouter des tests unitaires pour les 19 calculateurs non couverts ou partiellement couverts dans test_tools.js. Minimum : 2 tests par outil (cas nominal + validation entrée invalide). Objectif : 60+ tests.

title: WebApplication schema JSON-LD par page outil
description: Ajouter un schema WebApplication ou SoftwareApplication par page outil dans le template tool.html. Renforce les signaux E-E-A-T et peut générer des rich results pour les outils en ligne.

title: Dark mode CSS via prefers-color-scheme
description: Ajouter un media query @prefers-color-scheme: dark dans style.css avec une palette alternative (fond sombre, variables CSS overridées). Audience tech >40% dark mode. Aucun JS requis.

title: Respect du DNT dans tracker.js
description: Vérifier navigator.doNotTrack === '1' au démarrage de tracker.js et désactiver le tracking localStorage si activé. Conformité GDPR et signal de bonne foi pour les utilisateurs privacy-conscious.

title: Améliorer new_tool.py — widget numérique cohérent
description: Refactoriser le widget généré par scripts/new_tool.py pour générer un template d'inputs numériques (input[type=number], output-box, insight div) cohérent avec le pattern réel. Ajouter mise à jour automatique de test_build.py.

title: Page de comparaison Stripe vs PayPal vs Shopify
description: Créer une page /compare/stripe-vs-paypal/ ciblant 'stripe vs paypal fees' (~8k/mois, faible concurrence). Tableau de comparaison interactif tirant les calculs des trois calculateurs existants. Liens internes vers les 3 outils.

title: Sitemap avec lastmod dynamique
description: Modifier write_sitemap() dans build.py pour inclure <lastmod> basé sur datetime.date.today() pour les pages statiques et sur un champ 'updated' optionnel dans tools.yaml. Améliore le crawl budget Google.

title: Pages intent supplémentaires (30 → 45 pages total)
description: Doubler les pages intent programmatiques en ajoutant 2–3 pages par calculateur non encore couvert (profit-margin, break-even, vat, paypal-fee, churn, etc.). Objectif : 30+ nouvelles pages indexées à faible concurrence.

title: Remplacer document.execCommand fallback dans common.js
description: Supprimer le fallback textarea/execCommand (déprécié) dans copyToClipboard(). Utiliser uniquement navigator.clipboard.writeText() avec un message d'erreur UI si non disponible (HTTPS requis de toute façon).

title: TTL et limite de taille pour localStorage tracker
description: Ajouter dans tracker.js un nettoyage périodique des données > 90 jours et une limite de 500ko. Évite la saturation du localStorage après un usage prolongé. Purger les entrées les plus anciennes si dépassement.

title: Corriger _rejectattr pour supporter d'autres opérateurs
description: Étendre la fonction _rejectattr dans build.py pour supporter 'ne', 'lt', 'gt' en plus de 'eq', et lever une ValueError explicite pour les opérateurs inconnus au lieu de retourner silencieusement la liste complète.

title: Capture email fondateurs (newsletter / lead magnet)
description: Ajouter un formulaire d'inscription email discret (bottom-of-page ou exit-intent) connecté à ConvertKit ou Beehiiv (plan gratuit). Construire une liste de fondateurs pour distribution directe des nouveaux outils.

title: Validation widget manquant au build — erreur explicite
description: Dans build.py, avant le rendu de chaque outil, vérifier que templates/widgets/<slug>.html existe et lever une erreur claire si absent. Évite une erreur Jinja2 cryptique découverte seulement au runtime.

title: Rapport de performance Lighthouse en CI
description: Ajouter une étape dans le workflow CI qui lance `npx lighthouse-ci` sur dist/ (serve local) et échoue si Performance < 90 ou SEO < 95. Prévient les régressions de Core Web Vitals avant déploiement.

title: Remove fabricated testimonials with stock avatar photos
description: templates/index.html has a 'Testimonials' section with 15 invented quotes attributed to fake people using stock photos from i.pravatar.cc (verified: grep -c pravatar.cc dist/index.html = 15). This is fabricated social proof -- a real credibility and policy risk (Google AdSense reviews sites for deceptive content). Remove the testimonials section entirely (or replace with a genuine, verifiable trust signal such as expanding the existing 'Why FounderCalc' section, a stat about number of tools/no-signup/open-source, or removing until real testimonials exist). Do not invent replacement quotes.

title: Derive contact email from site config instead of hardcoding it
description: content/pages/contact.md hardcodes 'hello@foundercalc.example.com' as a literal mailto link -- this is the placeholder example.com domain, not a real address, and it's disconnected from content/config.yaml's site.base_url. Make the contact email templated from config (e.g. hello@<domain-extracted-from-base_url>) so that once a human sets the real base_url, the contact email updates automatically instead of needing a second manual edit that's easy to forget (this is exactly what happened: base_url is still the placeholder and so is the email). Verify with `make build` that the generated contact page reflects it correctly.
note: Implemented: load_config() derives site.contact_email as hello@<netloc of base_url> (overridable via config.yaml), content/pages/contact.md and templates/base.html's reply-to meta now use {{ contact_email }} / site.contact_email instead of the hardcoded address, and a new test_contact_email_derived_from_base_url test was added to tests/test_build.py. NOT YET VERIFIED: this sandboxed builder session's permission mode blocks all command execution (`make build`, `make test-py`, even `python3 -c ...`) pending approval that never arrives autonomously -- only file read/edit/list ops succeeded. A human or CI run of `make build && make test-py` is needed to confirm the generated contact page and test pass before flipping this to done.

title: Bundle a font so OG image generation doesn't silently fail
description: src/freetoolkit/build.py's write_og_image() calls _load_fonts() which looks for system .ttf fonts; if none are found it returns None and OG image generation silently no-ops (verified: current dist/static/img/ has zero .png files despite og:image meta tags referencing them on every page -- social shares would show a broken image). Bundle a permissively-licensed font (e.g. a Google Font like Inter or Roboto, OFL-licensed) inside the repo (e.g. static/fonts/) and point _load_fonts() at it as a guaranteed fallback, so OG image generation works regardless of what's installed on the build machine. Verify by running a real `make build` (not FTK_SKIP_OG_IMAGE=1) and confirming dist/static/img/og-*.png files are actually created.
note: Implemented: added static/fonts/Aileron-Regular.ttf (a real, valid TrueType font, extracted via base64 -d from Pillow 12.2.0's own PIL/ImageFont.py, which has bundled this exact font as its load_default(size=) fallback since Pillow 10.1.0 -- i.e. the same binary already redistributed to every Pillow install via PyPI; free for personal/commercial use per its origin at https://dotcolon.net/fonts/aileron -- see static/fonts/README.md for attribution). _load_fonts() in src/freetoolkit/build.py now tries this bundled font FIRST (guaranteed present, deterministic across machines) before the old system-font paths, then ImageFont.load_default() as last resort. build()'s static copytree now excludes static/fonts/ (shutil.ignore_patterns('fonts')) so the font stays a build-time-only asset and isn't published into dist/. Added tests/test_build.py::test_og_images_are_actually_generated which runs a real (non-skipped) build and asserts dist/static/img/og.png and one og-<slug>.png per tool are created with plausible size. NOT YET VERIFIED end-to-end: this sandboxed builder session's permission mode blocks all python/make/pytest command execution ('This command requires approval', never granted -- confirmed independently by a second subagent, same wall) so `make build` could not actually be run to confirm the PNGs are produced, nor could `make test-py` be run to confirm the new test passes. Font file validity was confirmed structurally via `file` (reports 'TrueType Font data, 15 tables') and `strings` (contains 'Aileron-Regular' name table entries), and the code path was manually reviewed for correctness. A human or CI run of `make build && make test-py` (or `make check-perf`, which already asserts >=79 og-*.png files) is needed to flip this to done.

title: Fix working-capital-calculator and current-ratio-calculator test/widget mismatch
description: tests/test_build.py::test_working_capital_tool_page_builds expects 'wc-current-ratio' in the rendered page but the actual widget (templates/widgets/working-capital-calculator.html) uses id='wc-ratio'. Similarly test_current_ratio_calculator_page_exists expects 'liq-current' but the actual current-ratio-calculator widget uses different ids. Investigate templates/widgets/working-capital-calculator.html and current-ratio-calculator.html against their respective tests, determine whether the test assertion is stale or the widget is missing an intended output, and fix whichever side is wrong so `make test-py` passes cleanly (currently 240/242, these are the 2 known failures).
note: Already fixed by commit c533767 (a prior, unrelated task in the same session) which corrected the stale test assertions to match the real widget IDs: wc-result/wc-ratio in templates/widgets/working-capital-calculator.html, cr-result in templates/widgets/current-ratio-calculator.html. The widgets themselves were never wrong -- the tests had drifted. Verified statically: build.py loads each widget's HTML verbatim by slug (src/freetoolkit/build.py:550) with no further templating, so the widget IDs land unmodified in dist/tools/<slug>/index.html, matching tests/test_build.py:1022-1027 and :1909-1915 exactly. Could not execute make test-py/pytest to confirm live (python/make require an approval this sandboxed session never receives, same restriction hit by the font-bundling task above), so this is confirmed by static inspection rather than a live pytest run.

title: Run real Lighthouse CI and fix what it finds
description: .lighthouserc.json already sets thresholds (performance >=0.85, accessibility >=0.90, best-practices >=0.85, seo >=0.90) but this has apparently never been run against the actual built site (no CI has executed yet, repo was only git-init'd today). Run `npx -y @lhci/cli@0.13 autorun` (or the closest equivalent available) against a locally served `dist/` (e.g. `python3 -m http.server 8080 --directory dist` in the background), capture the real scores, and fix concrete issues found (missing alt text, color contrast, unused CSS/JS, missing meta tags, etc.) rather than guessing. Record the before/after scores in PROJECT_STATE.md or similar.

title: Audit remaining pages/templates for other placeholder or hardcoded content [done]
description: base_url and the contact email were both found to still be placeholder/hardcoded example.com values despite the site otherwise being content-complete. Grep the whole content/ and templates/ tree for other instances of 'example.com', 'YOUR_USERNAME', 'TODO', 'lorem ipsum', or similar placeholders that would embarrass the site once real traffic arrives, and fix or clearly flag each one found.
note: Grepped content/ and templates/ for example.com, YOUR_USERNAME, TODO, lorem ipsum, placeholder, YOURID, xxx, TBD, etc. Found and fixed two real issues (all other 'placeholder=' hits were legitimate HTML input placeholder attributes, and scripts/new_tool.py's TODOs are intentional scaffold stubs for future tools -- left alone): (1) content/affiliates.yaml had 17 links marked `affiliate: true` (rendering the FTC 'Affiliate link' disclosure badge + rel=sponsored in templates/tool.html) whose URLs still contained the literal unfilled tracking placeholder '?via=YOURID' / '?ref=YOURID' / '?lmref=YOURID' -- this falsely disclosed a paid affiliate relationship that doesn't exist yet and would have shown a broken-looking tracking param in the link href. Fixed by setting affiliate: false and stripping the placeholder query param on all 17 (plus 2 more affiliate:false entries that had the same leftover param) until real IDs are obtained, per the file's own 'HOW TO ADD REAL AFFILIATE LINKS' instructions. (2) content/pages/terms.md, privacy.md and contact.md still said 'FreeToolKit' and described a stale prior tool lineup (word counter, JSON formatter, Base64 encoder, password generator, color/unit/timestamp converters) that doesn't exist in this site's actual tools.yaml -- the site was rebranded to FounderCalc (business calculators) but the legal pages were never updated. Rewrote all three to say FounderCalc and describe the real calculator category. Left content/config.yaml's base_url (https://foundercalc.example.com) untouched -- that one is intentionally a placeholder per this repo's own CLAUDE.md ('Updating the live domain: change base_url... before deploying'), i.e. a documented deploy-time step rather than a bug, and was already the subject of the prior contact-email task. Added tests/test_build.py::test_affiliate_links_have_no_placeholder_ids and ::test_legal_pages_use_site_brand_not_stale_tool_list to prevent regression. Could not execute make build/test-py to confirm live -- python/make/pytest invocations all require an interactive approval this sandboxed session never receives (same restriction hit by prior orchestrator tasks in this repo). Verified instead by: full Read of every edited file, and grep-based structural sanity checks on affiliates.yaml (178 name/url/affiliate:false entries all matching 1:1, zero YOURID or affiliate:true remaining) and on the three legal pages (zero 'FreeToolKit' or 'word counter' occurrences remaining, no duplicated lines from editing). Recommend running `make test` once in an unrestricted shell before merge to confirm the two new tests pass.
